revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=とうかん山古墳| 所在地=埼玉県熊谷市箕輪| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長74m、後円部径37m、前方部幅42m、高さ6.2m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃から後半| 出土品=埴輪| 史跡指定=[[平成]]元年（[[1989年]]）県指定| '''とうかん山古墳'''（とうかんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]箕輪にある[[前方後円墳]]である。
==概要== 港南台地上、標高24メートルの地点に築造された。墳丘は宅地造成によって一部変形しているが、同地に残された唯一形のわかる前方後円墳である。正式な発掘調査はされておらず、墳丘で採集された埴輪片から、6世紀中頃から後半の築造と推定されている。
== 参考文献 == 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳・大里』 さきたま出版会 2004年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:とうかんやまこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=とうかん山古墳| 所在地=埼玉県熊谷市箕輪| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長74m、後円部径37m、前方部幅42m、高さ6.2m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃から後半| 出土品=埴輪| 史跡指定=[[平成]]元年（[[1989年]]）県指定| }}
'''とうかん山古墳'''（とうかんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]箕輪にある[[前方後円墳]]である。
==概要== 港南台地上、標高24メートルの地点に築造された。墳丘は宅地造成によって一部変形しているが、同地に残された唯一形のわかる前方後円墳である。正式な発掘調査はされておらず、墳丘で採集された埴輪片から、6世紀中頃から後半の築造と推定されている。
== 参考文献 == 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳・大里』 さきたま出版会 2004年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]

== 参考文献 == 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳・大里』 さきたま出版会 2004年

revision.comment: 1個の出典を修正し、0個にリンク切れのタグを追加しました。 #IABot (v1.6.5)
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=とうかん山古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Kumagaya_Tokanyama_Tumulus_1.JPG|250px]]| 画像キャプション=とうかん山古墳（2011年5月）| 所在地=埼玉県熊谷市箕輪| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|5|13.4|N|139|24|43|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長74m、後円部径37m、高さ6.2m、前方部幅42m、高さ5.7m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃から後半| 出土品=埴輪| 史跡指定=[[平成]]元年（[[1989年]]）県指定| }}
'''とうかん山古墳'''（とうかんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]箕輪にある[[前方後円墳]]である。港南台地上、標高24メートルの地点に築造された。墳丘は宅地造成によって一部変形しているが、同地に残された唯一形のわかる前方後円墳である。正式な発掘調査はされておらず、墳丘で採集された埴輪片から、6世紀中頃から後半の築造と推定されている。古墳名は墳頂にある稲荷社(おとうか様)や十日夜碑に由来する。
<gallery> 画像:Kumagaya Tokanyama Tumulus 2.JPG|全景 </gallery>
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳・大里』さきたま出版会、2004年、369－370頁
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県指定文化財一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20131006155245/http://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/about/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html とうかん山古墳] - 熊谷市
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:とうかんやまこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = とうかん山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]箕輪99-1ほか |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 5|緯度秒 = 13.58 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 24|経度秒 = 43.10 |ISO = JP-11 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6.2m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 埼玉県指定史跡「とうかん山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=とうかん山
古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=5|lat_sec=13.58|lon_deg=139|lon_min=24|lon_sec=43.10|mark=前方後円墳-南西.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''とうかん山古墳'''（とうかんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]箕輪にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。埼玉県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 埼玉県中部、比企丘陵北辺部の台地中央部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|箕輪村（平凡社）|2004}}。 墳丘上には稲荷社（おとうか様）・十日夜碑が所在し、古墳名はこれらに由来する。現在までに墳丘周囲は改変を受けているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける史跡説明板。。墳丘長75メートルを測り、熊谷市内では最大規模、[[埼玉古墳群]]（[[行田市]]）との比較では[[瓦塚古墳]]と同程度の規模の前方後円墳になる。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]が採集されている。また墳丘周囲には周溝が巡ると見られるが、現在は埋没している。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[横穴式石室]]と推定される。副葬品は詳らかでない。 このとうかん山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm とうかん山古墳]（熊谷市立江南文化財センター「熊谷デジタルミュージアム」）。（または6世紀中葉）頃の築造と推定される。比企丘陵北端の大古墳群である[[三千塚古墳群]]の東端に所在するとともに、南約1キロメートルに所在する[[甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|甲山古墳]]（熊谷市冑山）と合わせて[[埼玉古墳群]]とも関係する大首長の存在を示唆するとして重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に埼玉県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：75メートル（または74メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：37メートル ** 高さ：6.2メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：38メートル File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|前方部墳頂の稲荷神社 File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|前方部墳丘 == 文化財 == === 埼玉県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** とうかん山古墳 - 所有者は保安寺。1989年（平成元年）3月17日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf 埼玉県指定史跡]}}（埼玉県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（埼玉県教育委員会・大里村教育委員会、1991年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=箕輪村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 11 埼玉県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910300|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|2004}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=塩野博|year=2004|chapter=|title=埼玉の古墳 大里|publisher=さきたま出版会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[甲山古墳 (熊谷市)]] * [[瓦塚古墳]] * [[三千塚古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html とうかん山古墳] - 熊谷市ホームページ * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm とうかん山古墳] - 熊谷市立江南文化財センター「熊谷デジタルミュージアム」 {{デフォルトソート:とうかんやまこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]]

古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.58|lon_deg=139|lon_min=24|lon_sec=43.10|mark=前方後円墳-南西.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=埼玉県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|05|13.58|N|139|24|43.10|E}}}} }} '''とうかん山古墳'''（とうかんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]箕輪にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。埼玉県指定[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 埼玉県中部、比企丘陵北辺部の台地中央部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}。墳丘上には稲荷社（おとうか様）・十日夜碑が所在し、古墳名はこれらに由来する。現在までに墳丘周囲は改変を受けているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける史跡説明板。。墳丘長75メートルを測り、熊谷市内では最大規模、[[埼玉古墳群]]（[[行田市]]）との比較では[[瓦塚古墳]]と同程度の規模の前方後円墳になる。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]が採集されている。また墳丘周囲には周溝が巡ると見られるが、現在は埋没している。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[横穴式石室]]と推定される。副葬品は詳らかでない。 このとうかん山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm とうかん山古墳]（熊谷市立江南文化財センター「熊谷デジタルミュージアム」）。（または6世紀中葉）頃の築造と推定される。比企丘陵北端の大古墳群である[[三千塚古墳群]]の東端に所在するとともに、南約1キロメートルに所在する[[甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|甲山古墳]]（熊谷市冑山）と合わせて[[埼玉古墳群]]とも関係する大首長の存在を示唆するとして重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に埼玉県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：75メートル（または74メートル） * 後円部 ** 直径：37メートル ** 高さ：6.2メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：38メートル File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|前方部墳頂の稲荷神社 File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|前方部墳丘 == 文化財 == === 埼玉県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** とうかん山古墳 - 所有者は保安寺。1989年（平成元年）3月17日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf 埼玉県指定史跡]}}（埼玉県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（埼玉県教育委員会・大里村教育委員会、1991年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=箕輪村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 11 埼玉県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=塩野博|year=2004|chapter=|title=埼玉の古墳 大里|publisher=さきたま出版会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[甲山古墳 (熊谷市)]] * [[瓦塚古墳]] * [[三千塚古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html とうかん山古墳] - 熊谷市ホームページ * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm とうかん山古墳] - 熊谷市立江南文化財センター「熊谷デジタルミュージアム」 {{デフォルトソート:とうかんやまこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]]

所有者は保安寺。1989年（平成元年）3月17日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf 埼玉県指定史跡]}}（埼玉県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（埼玉県教育委員会・大里村教育委員会、1991年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=箕輪村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 11 埼玉県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=塩野博|year=2004|chapter=|title=埼玉の古墳 大里|publisher=さきたま出版会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[甲山古墳 (熊谷市)]] * [[瓦塚古墳]] * [[三千塚古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html とうかん山古墳] - 熊谷市ホームページ * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm とうかん山古墳] - 熊谷市立江南文化財センター「熊谷デジタルミュージアム」 {{デフォルトソート:とうかんやまこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Saitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"
{{DEFAULTSORT:Tōkan-yama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumagaya History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]]

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Saitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Saitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.

The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>

Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.

The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

{{DEFAULTSORT:Tōkan-yama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumagaya History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[ja:%E3%81%A8%E3%81%86%E3%81%8B%E3%82%93%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[ja:&action=edit]]

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

revision.text:
'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"
{{DEFAULTSORT::Tōkan-yama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumagaya History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E3%81%A8%E3%81%86%E3%81%8B%E3%82%93%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

revision.text:
'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"
{{citation needed|Tōkan-yama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumagaya History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tōkanyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tōkanyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:01-17]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tōkanyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[ja:%E3%81%A8%E3%81%86%E3%81%8B%E3%82%93%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tōkanyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:01-17]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Tōkanyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tōkanyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tōkanyama Kofun]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.

Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|ja|瓦塚古墳}} * {{ill|Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Minowa, Kumagaya|ja|箕輪}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Tumulus Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Tumulus length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
