The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>

== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture
revision.text:
'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Minowa|Minowa, Kumagaya]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

[[sn:Tōkanyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E3%81%A8%E3%81%86%E3%81%8B%E3%82%93%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Tōkanyama Kofun]] [[ja:&action=edit]]
<!--Tōkanyama Kofun--><!--Tōkanyama Kofun--><!--Tōkanyama Kofun--><!--Tōkanyama Kofun--><!--Tōkanyama Kofun--> [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]]

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Minowa|Minowa, Kumagaya]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

'''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Minowa|Minowa, Kumagaya]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōkan-yama Kofun''' (とうかんやま古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Minowa|Minowa, Kumagaya]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The Tōkan-yama Kofun is situated in central Hiki Plateau in central Saitama Prefecture. The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>

Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

It is designated as a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site.

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.

Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>

Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"

The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>

While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>

The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"
{{translated page|ja|とうかん山古墳|version=96036259|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The tumulus gets its name from the Inari Shrine (referred to locally as "Otōka-sama") and the Tōkanya Monument located on the mound. While parts of the mound have been altered over time, no archaeological excavation has been conducted.
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing southwest.<ref name="説明板">Historical Site Explanation Board.</ref> Measuring 75 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in Kumagaya City and comparable in size to the Kawarazuka Kofun in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] located in Gyōda.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments have been collected from the mound surface.<ref name="説明板"/> The mound is believed to have had a surrounding moat, although it is now buried.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The burial facility remains undetermined but is presumed to be a {{ill|Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|lt=Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|en|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-entry horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> The nature of grave goods is not well understood.
The Tōkan-yama Kofun is thought to have been constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|古墳時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 6th century),<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム">[http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] (Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum").</ref><ref name="一覧"/> or possibly mid-6th century.<ref name="説明板"/> It is situated on the eastern edge of the large Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster at the northern end of the Hiki Plateau and, along with the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|lt=Kabutoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} located about 1 kilometer to the north, is considered to indicate the presence of a significant regional leader connected to the Saitama Kofun Cluster.
The tumulus was designated a Saitama Prefecture Historic Site in 1989 (Heisei 1).<ref name="一覧"/>
== Kofun Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="熊谷市デジタルミュージアム"/> * Kofun length: 75 meters (alternatively 74 meters<ref name="説明板"/>) * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 37 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 38 meters <gallery> File:Tokan-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Tokan-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Tokan-yama Kofun, inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine at the apex of the front section File:Tokan-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Tōkan-yama Kofun - Owned by Hoan-ji Temple. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="一覧">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefecture Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture Homepage).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Saitama Prefectural Board of Education and Ōsato Village Board of Education, 1991) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Minowa Village|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 11 Saitama Prefecture Names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490115|ref={{Harvid|箕輪村（平凡社）|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hiroshi Shiono|year=2004|chapter=|title=Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|ja|甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)|qq=}} * [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhozuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokan-yama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/toukannyama.html Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya City Homepage * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/038bunka.htm Tōkan-yama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Kōnan Cultural Heritage Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"
